sections.paragraph:
(from אָנַהּ, Arab. أَنَهَ) i.q. אָנַח, אָנַק, אָנַן onomat. to groan, to sigh, نchzen, ftohnen; Isa 3:26 19:8 . Hence is derived the noun אֲנִיָּה Gr. ἀνία (ἀνιάω, ἀνιάζω), and תַּאֲנִיָּה.

II. אָנָה not used in Kal, to approach, to meet, to be present. Arab. أَنَى to be in time, إِنًى fit time. Conj. V, X. to delay, to have patience (prop. to give oneself time), to hold back.

Piel, to cause anything to happen to any one, or to meet with any one (used of God), Exo 21:13.

Pual, to be caused to meet, i.e. to light upon, to befall (von Gott zugefchickt erhalten), as misfortune, Pro 12:21 Psa 91:10.

Hithpael, to seek occasion for hurting another; construed with לְ 2Ki 5:7.

Derivatives אֳנִי, אֳנִיָּה, אֵת No. II. for אֵנֶת, תַּאֲנָה, תֹּאֲנָה.

אנה

whither? see אָן .
